notes:
Aquapolis (e-Card series, WotC, 2003). Booster pack = 9 cards (NOT the WotC 11-card layout); booster box = 36 packs; NO ETB (era predates ETBs). Confirmed pack structure across multiple corroborating sources (Bulbapedia-derived snippet, PSA Set Registry, Serebii, Flipside): 1 rare + 2 uncommons + 5 commons + 1 reverse-holo. A regular HOLO rare appears in ~1 of every 3 packs as an EXTRA card that replaces a common (so the pack still has a guaranteed non-holo rare in the rare slot). Crystal SECRET-RARE cards (3 in set: Nidoking, Kingdra, Lugia) pull at ~1:36 packs (~1 per box) per Flipside/PSA — these are NOT available as reverse holos.

Bucket mapping (keys are a subset of {rare,uncommon,common,secret-rare,rare-holo}):
- 'Rare slot' (count 1, the guaranteed rare-or-better): rare-holo = 1/3 ≈ 0.3333 (holo appears ~1:3 packs and is the chase hit; modeled as occupying/upgrading the rare slot to keep slots disjoint), secret-rare (crystal) = 1/36 ≈ 0.0278, plain rare = remainder 0.6389. Sums to 1.0. Hit bucket = rare-holo + secret-rare ≈ 0.361 per pack.
- 'Uncommon slot' (count 2): all uncommon.
- 'Common slot' (count 5): all common.
- 'Reverse holo slot' (count 1): per era baseline, reverse holo filler is any common/uncommon (crystals & holos excluded) → modeled 50/50 common/uncommon.

confidence=medium: pack composition + holo (1:3) + crystal (1:36) rates are from consistent community/collector documentation, but there is no large per-set statistical pull study for this vintage set. Note: a vintage Aquapolis sealed BOX carries a large collector/sealed premium, so computed EV/pack will sit well below box price — expected for WotC-era product, rates NOT inflated to compensate.
